summary.workflow_impact: Generation pipeline blocked; users cannot complete diagnosis→config→generation flow

errors.name: Light Period Undefined Runtime Error
errors.error_message: Uncaught TypeError: can't access property "replace", result.light.period is undefined

root_cause.primary: Vision API returns incomplete light object with period field as null/undefined
secondary: Optional chaining fails at nested property level before fallback evaluates
No normalization applied to incomplete API responses
root_cause.why_occurs: When Vision API confidence in lighting detection is low (ambiguous images, floor plans, technical drawings), it returns a partial light object. The result.light?.period?.replace() chain crashes because period is undefined, and the || 'N/A' fallback never executes due to error occurring earlier in chain.

data_flow.api_response: Returns ScanResult with incomplete light object
data_flow.error_propagation: Uncaught error → React error boundary (if configured) or crash
data_flow.user_impact: Cannot view diagnosis, cannot advance workflow

impact.user_impact: Crash prevents viewing diagnostic results and progression to configuration
impact.scope: Diagnosis display phase, blocks config/generation steps
affected_workflows: Viewing scan results in DiagnosisStep component
affected_user_segments: Users uploading floor plans (low lighting detail)

triggers: User uploads technical drawing without perspective
User uploads poorly lit or ambiguous image
Vision API returns partial light analysis (confidence < 0.5)
ScanResult validation passes with null period

fix_options.title: Implement Light Normalization
fix_options.description: Add normalizeLight() in response-normalizer.ts to apply defaults to incomplete responses

implementation.approach: Apply defaults for all nullish fields

fix_options.title: Strict Schema Validation
fix_options.caveat: May break existing Vision API responses if they return null period

errors.name: JSON Schema Validation Failure - Reserved Keyword
errors.error_message: KIE API Error Response: Object { code: 422, msg: "$.response_format.json_schema.schema.properties.blocks.items.properties.type must be string or array", data: null }

root_cause.primary: Property named 'type' in JSON schema conflicts with JSON Schema reserved keyword 'type'
secondary: Strict mode (line 1104) enforces RFC 8927 / JSON Schema Draft 2020-12
Schema only defines type+content; missing required fields from prompt specification
System prompt expects 'block_type' but schema defines 'type'
root_cause.why_occurs: JSON Schema specification designates 'type' as a keyword for defining value types. When strict mode is enabled, API validation rejects any property literally named 'type' as it violates schema specification. The resolver expects a JSON Schema type definition (string|array|object|etc) but finds a property definition instead.

affected_code.code: response_format: { type: 'json_schema', json_schema: { strict: true, schema: { ... properties: { type: { type: 'string' } } } } }

affected_code.problem: Property name 'type' is reserved in JSON Schema
Strict mode enforces RFC compliance - rejects schema with reserved keywords
Prompt instructions specify 'block_type' but schema uses 'type' - mismatch

data_flow.entry_point: User clicks Generate Prompt button in ResultStep
data_flow.request_building: Axios POST request with json_schema response_format
data_flow.api_validation: Gemini API validates schema against JSON Schema Draft 2020-12
data_flow.validation_failure: Parser detects 'type' is reserved keyword at $.response_format.json_schema.schema.properties.blocks.items.properties.type
data_flow.error_response: 422 Unprocessable Entity with message about reserved keyword
data_flow.error_handling: catch block (line 1157) logs error and rethrows
data_flow.user_impact: Promise rejection, error shown to user, generation blocked

impact.user_impact: Users cannot generate blocks-mode prompts; fallback to single mode only
impact.scope: Blocks mode prompt generation only (single mode unaffected)
affected_user_segments: Users selecting blocks mode (100% of blocks mode users)

triggers: Axios sends POST to Gemini API
API validates schema in strict mode
API schema parser encounters 'type' property name
Parser checks if 'type' is JSON Schema keyword

technical_details.why_invalid: Property named 'type' must contain a JSON Schema type value (string|number|object|array|boolean|null), not a property definition. Strict mode doesn't allow this pattern.

schema_mismatch.mismatch_impact: Schema and prompt instructions don't align; API may receive unexpected field names

missing_schema_fields.reason: Prompt requires this field - not defined in schema
Prompt specifies titles for blocks - not defined
Prompt requests word count - not defined
Prompt requests engine recommendation - not defined
Prompt requests quality score per block - not defined
Prompt requests detailed breakdown - not defined

fix_options.title: Rename Property from 'type' to 'block_type'
fix_options.description: Change reserved keyword 'type' to custom field name 'block_type' in schema

fix_options.title: Expand Schema with All Required Fields
fix_options.description: Add all fields specified in prompt (title, word_count, engine_recommendation, quality_score, quality_breakdown)

implementation.action: Replace schema definition with complete field list

fix_options.title: Update Code Accessing block_type Field
fix_options.description: Ensure all code using blocks expects 'block_type' not 'type'

fix_options.title: Validate Schema Before Deployment
fix_options.description: Test updated schema against Gemini API mock or staging

error_chain_analysis.relationship: Independent but concurrent - both block different steps of same workflow

dependency_graph.combined_impact: Users cannot complete workflow due to both diagnosis display AND prompt generation failures

workflow_impact.user_can_proceed: Only if ERR_001 does not occur AND using single mode (avoiding ERR_002)

priority_2.action: Add conditional check for period before calling replace()

priority_3.action: Expand blocks schema with all required fields

priority_4.action: Implement normalizeLight() function for systematic handling

priority_5.action: Add comprehensive unit and integration tests

cases.name: should display N/A when period undefined
should not use reserved keywords as property names
cases.validation: Check schema properties against reserved list

cases.name: should define block_type not type
cases.expected: block_type field present, type field absent

integration_tests.scenario: Vision API returns incomplete light object
steps: Mock API to return { light: { period: null, temp_k: 5500 } }
Verify result can be displayed without crash
Verify all required fields have defaults
Verify API request schema is valid

manual_testing: Upload floor plan → verify diagnosis displays without crash
Upload technical drawing → verify light period shows 'N/A' safely
Select blocks mode → verify Generate button works
Verify API returns blocks with block_type field

files_affected.changes_needed: Add null checks for light.period and light.dominant_source
Rename type to block_type, expand schema, verify consistency
Verify blocks processing uses block_type not type

additional_notes.vision_api_limitations: Vision API sometimes returns incomplete light data for floor plans and technical drawings due to lack of perspective cues
additional_notes.gemini_api_strict_mode: Strict mode enforces RFC 8927 (JSON Schema 2020-12) compliance, rejecting schemas with reserved keywords
additional_notes.user_experience: Users see crashes (ERR_001) or error messages (ERR_002), blocking completion of generation workflow
additional_notes.regression_risk: Both fixes are minimal; risk of regression is low if tested properly

references.rfc_8927: JSON Schema: A Media Type for Describing JSON Documents
